title: ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. vulgatum
intro: Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. vulgatum, also known as the adder's tongue fern, is a plant species belonging to the family Ophioglossaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. vulgatum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant activity, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Ophioglossum vulgatum subsp. vulgatum can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or salve, and is often used topically to treat skin issues and wounds, as well as internally to support overall health and well-being.
